features.text: **Spend a Fear** to weigh down the spirits of all PCs within Far range. All targets affected replace their Hope Die with a **d8** until they roll a success with Hope or their next rest.

features.name: Your Friends Will Fail You
features.text: When a PC fails with Fear, you can **mark a Stress** to cause all other PCs within Close range to lose a Hope.

features.name: Momentum
features.text: When the Demon makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

adversaries.name: Demon of Hubris
adversaries.description: A perfectly beautiful and infinitely cruel demon with a gleaming spear and elegant robes.
adversaries.motives: Condescend, declare premature victory, prove superiority

features.name: Terrifying
features.text: When the Demon makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range must lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Double or Nothing
features.text: When a PC within Far range fails a roll, they can choose to reroll their Fear Die and take the new result. If they still fail, they mark 2 Stress and the Demon clears a Stress.

features.name: Unparalleled Skill
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to deal the Demon's standard attack damage to a target within Close range.

features.name: The Root of Villainy
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to spotlight two other Demons within Far range.

features.name: You Pale in Comparison
features.text: When a PC fails a roll within Close range of the Demon, they must mark a Stress.

adversaries.name: Demon of Jealousy
adversaries.description: A fickle creature of spindly limbs and insatiable desires.
adversaries.motives: Join in on others' success, take what belongs to others, hold grudges

features.name: Unprotected Mind
features.text: The Demon's standard attack deals direct damage.

features.name: My Turn
features.text: When the Demon marks HP from an attack, **spend a number of Fear equal to the HP marked by the Demon** to cause the attacker to mark the same number of HP.

features.name: Rivalry
features.text: When a creature within Close range takes damage from a different adversary, you can **mark a Stress** to add a **d4** to the damage roll.

features.name: What's Yours Is Mine
features.text: When a PC takes Severe damage within Very Close range of the Demon, you can **spend a Fear** to cause the target to make a Finesse Reaction Roll. On a failure, the Demon seizes one item or consumable of their choice from the target's inventory.

adversaries.name: Demon of Wrath
adversaries.description: A hulking demon with boulder-sized fists, driven by endless rage.
adversaries.motives: Fuel anger, impress rivals, wreak havoc

features.name: Anger Unrelenting
features.text: The Demon's attacks deal direct damage.

features.name: Battle Lust
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to boil the blood of all PCs within Far range. They use a d20 as their Fear Die until the end of the scene.

features.name: Retaliation
features.text: When the Demon takes damage from an attack within Close range, you can **mark a Stress** to make a standard attack against the attacker.

features.name: Blood and Souls
features.text: Activate the first time an attack is made within sight of the Demon. It ticks down when a PC takes a violent action. When it triggers, summon **1d4** Minor Demons, who appear at Close range.

adversaries.name: Dire Bat
adversaries.description: A wide-winged pet endlessly loyal to their vampire owner.
adversaries.motives: Dive-bomb, hide, protect leader

features.name: Flying
features.text: While flying, the Bat gains a +3 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Screech
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to send a high-pitch screech out toward all targets in front of the Bat within Far range. Those targets must mark **1d4** Stress.

features.name: Guardian
features.text: When an allied Vampire marks HP, you can **mark a Stress** to fly into Melee range of the attacker and make an attack with advantage against them. On a success, deal **2d6+2** physical damage.

adversaries.name: Dryad
adversaries.description: A nature spirit in the form of a humanoid tree.
adversaries.motives: Command, cultivate, drive out, preserve the forest

features.name: Bramble Patch
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to target a point within Far range. Create a patch of thorns that covers an area within Close range of that point. All targets within that area take **2d6+2** physical damage when they act. A target must succeed on a Finesse Roll or deal more than 20 damage to the Dryad with an attack to leave the area.

features.name: Grow Saplings
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to grow three Treant Sapling Minions, who appear at Close range and immediately take the spotlight.

features.name: We Are All One
features.text: When an ally dies within Close range, you can **spend a Fear** to clear 2 HP and 2 Stress as the fallen ally's life force is returned to the forest.

adversaries.name: Elemental Spark
adversaries.description: A blazing mote of elemental fire.
adversaries.motives: Blast, consume, gain mass

features.name: Minion (9)
features.text: The Elemental is defeated when they take any damage. For every 9 damage a PC deals to the Elemental, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to choose a target and spotlight all Elemental Sparks within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 5 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

adversaries.name: Greater Earth Elemental
adversaries.description: A living landslide of boulders and dust, as large as a house.
adversaries.motives: Avalanche, knock over, pummel

features.name: Slow
features.text: When you spotlight the Elemental and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they're preparing to do. When you spotlight the Elemental and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can act.

features.name: Crushing Blows
features.text: When the Elemental makes a successful attack, the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can't mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP.

features.name: Immovable Object
features.text: An attack that would move the Elemental moves them two fewer ranges (for example, Far becomes Very Close). When the Elemental takes physical damage, reduce it by 7.

features.name: Rockslide
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to create a rockslide that buries the land in front of Elemental within Close range with rockfall. All targets in this area must make an Agility Reaction Roll (19). Targets who fail take **2d12+5** physical damage and become _Vulnerable_ until their next roll with Hope. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.text: When the Elemental makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

adversaries.name: Greater Water Elemental
adversaries.description: A huge living wave that crashes down upon enemies.
adversaries.motives: Deluge, disperse, drown

features.name: Water Jet
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to attack a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal **2d4+7** physical damage and the target's next action has disadvantage. On a failure, the target must mark a Stress.

features.name: Drowning Embrace
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Elemental succeeds against become _Restrained_ and _Vulnerable_ as they begin drowning. A target can break free, ending both conditions, with a successful Strength or Instinct Roll.

features.name: High Tide
features.text: When the Elemental makes a successful standard attack, you can **mark a Stress** to knock the target back to Close range.

adversaries.name: Head Vampire
adversaries.description: A captivating undead dressed in aristocratic finery.
adversaries.motives: Create thralls, charm, command, fly, intimidate

features.name: Terrifying
features.text: When the Vampire makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Look into My Eyes
features.text: A creature who moves into Melee range of the Vampire must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, you gain **1d4** Fear.

features.name: Feed on Followers
features.text: When the Vampire is within Melee range of an ally, they can cause the ally to mark a HP. The Vampire then clears a HP.

features.name: The Hunt Is On
features.text: **Spend 2 Fear** to summon **1d4** Vampires, who appear at Far range and immediately take the spotlight.

features.name: Lifesuck
features.text: When the Vampire is spotlighted, roll a **d8**. On a result of 6 or higher, all targets within Very Close range must mark a HP.

adversaries.name: Huge Green Ooze
adversaries.description: A translucent green mound of acid taller than most humans.
adversaries.motives: Camouflage, creep up, envelop, multiply

features.name: Slow
features.text: When you spotlight the Ooze and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they're preparing to do. When you spotlight the Ooze and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can act.

features.name: Acidic Form
features.text: When the Ooze makes a successful attack, the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can't mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP.

features.name: Envelop
features.text: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the Ooze _Envelops_ them and the target must mark 2 Stress. While _Enveloped_, the target must mark an additional Stress every time they make an action roll. When the Ooze takes Severe damage, all _Enveloped_ targets are freed and the condition is cleared.

features.name: Split
features.text: When the Ooze has 4 or more HP marked, you can **spend a Fear** to split them into two Green Oozes (with no marked HP or Stress). Immediately spotlight both of them.

adversaries.name: Hydra
adversaries.description: A quadrupedal scaled beast with multiple long-necked heads, each filled with menacing fangs.
adversaries.motives: Devour, regenerate, terrify

features.name: Many-Headed Menace
features.text: The Hydra begins with three heads and can have up to five. When the Hydra takes Major or greater damage, they lose a head.

features.name: Relentless (X)
features.text: The Hydra can be spotlighted X times per GM turn, where X is the Hydra's number of heads. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Regeneration
features.text: If the Hydra has any marked HP, **spend a Fear** to clear a HP and grow two heads.

features.name: Terrifying Chorus
features.text: All PCs within Far range lose 2 Hope.

features.name: Magical Weakness
features.text: When the Hydra takes magic damage, they become _Dazed_ until the next roll with Fear. While _Dazed_, they can't use their Regeneration action but are immune to magic damage.

adversaries.name: Monarch
adversaries.description: The sovereign ruler of a nation, wreathed in the privilege of tradition and wielding unmatched power in their domain.
adversaries.motives: Control vassals, destroy rivals, forge a legacy

features.name: Execute Them!
features.text: **Spend a Fear** per PC in the party to have the group condemned for crimes real or imagined. A PC who succeeds on a Presence Roll can demand trial by combat or another special form of trial.

features.name: Crownsguard
features.text: Once per scene, **mark a Stress** to summon six Tier 3 Minions, who appear at Close range to enforce the Monarch's will.

features.name: Casus Belli
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to activate after the Monarch's desire for war is first revealed. When it triggers, the Monarch has a reason to rally the nation to war and the support to act on that reason. You gain **1d4** Fear.

adversaries.name: Oak Treant
adversaries.description: A sturdy animate old-growth tree.
adversaries.motives: Hide in plain sight, preserve the forest, root down, swing branches

features.name: Just a Tree
features.text: Before they make their first attack in a fight or after they become _Hidden_, the Treant is indistinguishable from other trees until they next act or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll to identify them.

features.name: Seed Barrage
features.text: **Mark a Stress** and make an attack against up to three targets within Close range, pummeling them with giant acorns. Targets the Treant succeeds against take **2d10+5** physical damage.

features.name: Take Root
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to _Root_ the Treant in place. The Treant is _Restrained_ while _Rooted_, and can end this effect instead of moving while they are spotlighted. While Rooted, the Treant has resistance to physical damage.

adversaries.name: Stag Knight
adversaries.description: A knight with huge, majestic antlers wearing armor made of dangerous thorns.
adversaries.motives: Isolate, maneuver, protect the forest, weed the unwelcome

features.name: From Above
features.text: When the Knight succeeds on a standard attack from above a target, they deal **3d12+3** physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Blade of the Forest
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Knight succeeds against take physical damage equal to **3d4** + the target's Major threshold.

features.name: Thorny Armor
features.text: When the Knight takes damage from an attack within Melee range, you can **mark a Stress** to deal **1d10+5** physical damage to the attacker.

adversaries.name: Treant Sapling
adversaries.description: A small, sentient tree sapling.
adversaries.motives: Blend in, preserve the forest, pummel, surround

features.name: Minion (6)
features.text: The Sapling is defeated when they take any damage. For every 6 damage a PC deals to the Sapling, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to choose a target and spotlight all Treant Saplings within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 8 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

adversaries.name: Vampire
adversaries.description: An intelligent undead with blood-stained lips and a predator's smile.
adversaries.motives: Bite, charm, deceive, feed, intimidate

features.name: Draining Bite
features.text: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal **5d4** physical damage. A target who marks HP from this attack loses a Hope and must mark a Stress. The Vampire then clears a HP.

features.name: Mistform
features.text: When the Vampire takes physical damage, you can **spend a Fear** to take half damage.

adversaries.name: Vault Guardian Gaoler
adversaries.description: A boxy, dust-covered construct with thick metallic swinging doors on their torso.
adversaries.motives: Carry away, entrap, protect, pummel

features.name: Blocking Shield
features.text: Creatures within Melee range of the Gaoler have disadvantage on attack rolls against them. Creatures trapped inside the Gaoler are immune to this feature.

features.name: Lock Up
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target is _Restrained_ within the Gaoler until freed with a successful Strength Roll (18). While _Restrained_, the target can only attack the Gaoler.

adversaries.name: Vault Guardian Sentinel
adversaries.description: A dust-covered golden construct with boxy limbs and a huge mace for a hand.
adversaries.motives: Destroy at any cost, expunge, protect

features.name: Kinetic Slam
features.text: Targets who take damage from the Sentinel's standard attack are knocked back to Very Close range.

features.name: Box In
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to choose a target within Very Close range to focus on. That target has disadvantage on attack rolls when they're within Very Close range of the Sentinel. The Sentinel can only focus on one target at a time.

features.name: Mana Bolt
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to lob explosive magic at a point within Far range. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take **2d8+20** magic damage and are knocked back to Close range. Targets who succeed take half damage and aren't knocked back.

features.name: Momentum
features.text: When the Sentinel makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

adversaries.name: Vault Guardian Turret
adversaries.description: A massive living turret with reinforced armor and twelve pistondriven mechanical legs.
adversaries.motives: Concentrate fire, lock down, mark, protect

features.name: Slow Firing
features.text: When you spotlight the Turret and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't make a standard attack. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they're preparing to do. When you spotlight the Turret and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can attack.

features.name: Mark Target
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to _Mark_ a target within Far range until the Turret is destroyed or the _Marked_ target becomes _Hidden_. While the target is _Marked_, their Evasion is halved.

features.name: Concentrate Fire
features.text: When another adversary deals damage to a target within Far range of the Turret, you can **mark a Stress** to add the Turret's standard attack damage to the damage roll.

features.name: Detonation
features.text: When the Turret is destroyed, they explode. All targets within Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take **3d20** physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

adversaries.name: Young Ice Dragon
adversaries.description: A glacier-blue dragon with four powerful limbs and frost-tinged wings.
adversaries.motives: Avalanche, defend lair, fly, freeze, defend what is mine, maul

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.text: The Dragon can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Rend and Crush
features.text: If a target damaged by the Dragon doesn't mark an Armor Slot to reduce the damage, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: No Hope
features.text: When a PC rolls with Fear while within Far range of the Dragon, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Blizzard Breath
features.text: **Spend 2 Fear** to release an icy whorl in front of the Dragon within Close range. All targets in this area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take **4d6+5** magic damage and are _Restrained_ by ice until they break free with a successful Strength Roll. Targets who succeed must mark 2 Stress or take half damage.

features.name: Avalanche
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to have the Dragon unleash a huge downfall of snow and ice, covering all other creatures within Far range. All targets within this area must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or be buried in snow and rocks, becoming _Vulnerable_ until they dig themselves out from the debris. For each PC that fails the reaction roll, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Frozen Scales
features.text: When a creature makes a successful attack against the Dragon from within Very Close range, they must mark a Stress and become _Chilled_ until their next rest or they clear a Stress. While they are _Chilled_, they have disadvantage on attack rolls.

features.name: Momentum
features.text: When the Dragon makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

adversaries.name: Arch-Necromancer
adversaries.description: A decaying mage adorned in dark, tattered robes.
adversaries.motives: Corrupt, decay, flee to fight another day, resurrect

features.name: Dance of Death
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to spotlight **1d4** allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage, or full damage if you **spend a Fear**.

features.name: Beam of Decay
features.text: **Mark 2 Stress** to cause all targets within Far range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take **2d20+12** magic damage and you gain a Fear. Targets who succeed take half damage. A target who marks 2 or more HP must also mark **2 Stress** and becomes _Vulnerable_ until they roll with Hope.

features.name: Open the Gates of Death
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to summon a Zombie Legion, which appears at Close range and immediately takes the spotlight.

features.name: Not Today, My Dears
features.text: When the Necromancer has marked 7 or more of their HP, you can **spend a Fear** to have them teleport away to a safe location to recover. A PC who succeeds on an Instinct Roll can trace the teleportation magic to their destination.

features.name: Your Life Is Mine
features.text: When the Necromancer has marked 6 or more of their HP, activate the countdown. When it triggers, deal **2d10+6** direct magic damage to a target within Close range. The Necromancer then **clears a number of Stress or HP** equal to the number of HP marked by the target from this attack.

adversaries.name: Fallen Shock Troop
adversaries.description: A cursed soul bound to the Fallen's will.
adversaries.motives: Crush, dominate, earn relief, punish

features.name: Minion (12)
features.text: The Shock Troop is defeated when they take any damage. For every 12 damage a PC deals to the Shock Troop, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Aura of Doom
features.text: When a PC marks HP from an attack by the Shock Troop, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Group Attack
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to choose a target and spotlight all Fallen Shock Troops within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 12 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

adversaries.name: Fallen Sorcerer
adversaries.description: A powerful mage bound by the bargains they made in life.
adversaries.motives: Acquire, dishearten, dominate, torment

features.name: Conflagration
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to unleash an all-consuming firestorm and make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Sorcerer succeeds against take **2d10+6** direct magic damage.

features.name: Nightmare Tableau
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to trap a target within Far range in a powerful illusion of their worst fears. While trapped, the target is _Restrained_ and _Vulnerable_ until they break free, ending both conditions, with a successful Instinct Roll.

features.name: Slippery
features.text: When the Sorcerer takes damage from an attack, they can teleport up to Far range.

features.name: Shackles of Guilt
features.text: When the Sorcerer is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, all targets within Far range become _Vulnerable_ and must mark a Stress as they relive their greatest regrets. A target can break free from their regret with a successful Presence or Strength Roll. When a PC fails to break free, they lose a Hope.

adversaries.name: Fallen Warlord: Realm-Breaker
adversaries.description: A Fallen God, wreathed in rage and resentment, bearing millennia of experience in breaking heroes' spirits.
adversaries.motives: Corrupt, dominate, punish, break the weak

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.text: The Realm-Breaker can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Firespite Plate Armor
features.text: When the Realm-Breaker takes damage, reduce it by **2d10**.

features.name: Tormenting Lash
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to make a standard attack against all targets within Very Close range. When a target uses armor to reduce damage from this attack, they must mark 2 Armor Slots.

features.name: All-Consuming Rage
features.text: When the Realm-Breaker is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, create a torrent of incarnate rage that rends flesh from bone. All targets within Far range must make a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take **2d6+10** direct magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage. For each HP marked from this damage, summon a Fallen Shock Troop within Very Close range of the target who marked that HP. If the countdown ever decreases its maximum value to 0, the Realm-Breaker marks their remaining HP and all targets within Far range must mark all remaining HP and make a death move.

features.name: Doombringer
features.text: When a target marks HP from an attack by the Realm-Breaker, all PCs within Far range of the target must lose a Hope.

features.name: I Have Never Known Defeat (Phase Change)
features.text: When the Realm-Breaker marks their last HP, replace them with the Undefeated Champion and immediately spotlight them.

adversaries.name: Fallen Warlord: Undefeated Champion
adversaries.description: That which only the most feared have a chance to fear.
adversaries.motives: Dispatch merciless death, punish the defiant, secure victory at any cost

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.text: The Undefeated Champion can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Faltering Armor
features.text: When the Undefeated Champion takes damage, reduce it by **1d10**.

features.name: Shattering Strike
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to make a standard attack against all targets within Very Close range. PCs the Champion succeeds against lose a number of Hope equal to the HP they marked from this attack.

features.name: Endless Legions
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to summon a number of Fallen Shock Troops equal to twice the number of PCs. The Shock Troops appear at Far range.

features.name: Circle of Defilement
features.text: When the Undefeated Champion is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, activate a magical circle covering an area within Far range of the Champion. A target within that area is _Vulnerable_ until they leave the circle. The circle can be removed by dealing Severe damage to the Undefeated Champion.

features.name: Momentum
features.text: When the Undefeated Champion makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Doombringer
features.text: When a target marks HP from an attack by the Undefeated Champion, all PCs within Far range of the target lose a Hope.

adversaries.name: Hallowed Archer
adversaries.description: Spirit soldiers with sanctified bows.
adversaries.motives: Focus fire, obey, reposition, volley

features.name: Punish the Guilty
features.text: The Archer deals double damage to targets marked _Guilty_ by a High Seraph.

features.name: Divine Volley
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to make a standard attack against up to three targets.

adversaries.name: Hallowed Soldier
adversaries.description: Souls of the faithful, lifted up with divine weaponry.
adversaries.motives: Obey, outmaneuver, punish, swarm

features.name: Minion (13)
features.text: The Soldier is defeated when they take any damage. For every 13 damage a PC deals to the Soldier, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Divine Flight
features.text: While the Soldier is flying, **spend a Fear** to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Group Attack
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to choose a target and spotlight all Hallowed Soldiers within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 10 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

adversaries.name: High Seraph
adversaries.description: A divine champion, head of a hallowed host of warriors who enforce their god's will.
adversaries.motives: Enforce dogma, fly, pronounce judgment, smite

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.text: The Seraph can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Divine Flight
features.text: While the Seraph is flying, **spend a Fear** to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Judgment
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to make a target _Guilty_ in the eyes of the Seraph's god until the Seraph is defeated. While _Guilty_, the target doesn't gain Hope on a result with Hope. When the Seraph succeeds on a standard attack against a _Guilty_ target, they deal Severe damage instead of their standard damage. The Seraph can only mark one target at a time.

features.name: God Rays
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to reflect a sliver of divinity as a searing beam of light that hits up to twenty targets within Very Far range. Targets must make a Presence Reaction Roll, with disadvantage if they are marked _Guilty_. Targets who fail take **4d6+12** magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: We Are One
features.text: Once per scene, **spend a Fear** to spotlight all other adversaries within Far range. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

adversaries.name: Kraken
adversaries.description: A legendary beast of the sea, bigger than the largest galleon, with sucker-laden tentacles and a terrifying maw.
adversaries.motives: Consume, crush, drown, grapple

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.text: The Kraken can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Many Tentacles
features.text: While the Kraken has 7 or fewer marked HP, they can make their standard attack against two targets within range.

features.name: Grapple and Drown
features.text: Make an attack roll against a target within Close range. On a success, **mark a Stress** to grab them with a tentacle and drag them beneath the water. The target is _Restrained_ and _Vulnerable_ until they break free with a successful Strength Roll or the Kraken takes Major or greater damage. While _Restrained_ and _Vulnerable_ in this way, a target must mark a Stress when they make an action roll.

features.name: Boiling Blast
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to spew a line of boiling water at any number of targets in a line up to Far range. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take **4d6+9** physical damage. If a target marks an Armor Slot to reduce the damage, they must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.text: When the Kraken makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

adversaries.name: Oracle of Doom
adversaries.description: A towering immortal and incarnation of fate, cursed to only see bad outcomes.
adversaries.motives: Change environment, condemn, dishearten, toss aside

features.name: Terrifying
features.text: When the Oracle makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Walls Closing In
features.text: When a creature rolls a failure while within Very Far range of the Oracle, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Pronounce Fate
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to present a target within Far range with a vision of their personal nightmare. The target must make a Knowledge Reaction Roll. On a failure, they lose all Hope and take **2d20+4** direct magic damage. On a success, they take half damage and lose a Hope.

features.name: Summon Tormentors
features.text: Once per day, **spend 2 Fear** to summon **2d4** Tier 2 or below Minions relevant to one of the PC's personal nightmares. They appear at Close range relative to that PC.

features.name: Ominous Knowledge
features.text: When the Oracle sees a mortal creature, they instantly know one of their personal nightmares.

features.name: Vengeful Fate
features.text: When the Oracle marks HP from an attack within Very Close range, you can **mark a Stress** to knock the attacker back to Far range and deal **2d10+4** physical damage.

adversaries.name: Outer Realms Abomination
adversaries.description: A chaotic mockery of life, constantly in flux.
adversaries.motives: Demolish, devour, undermine

features.name: Chaotic Form
features.text: When the Abomination attacks, roll **2d4** and use the result as their attack modifier.

features.name: Disorienting Presence
features.text: When a target takes damage from the Abomination, they must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they gain disadvantage on their next action roll and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Reality Quake
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to rattle the edges of reality within Far range of the Abomination. All targets within that area must succeed on a Knowledge Reaction Roll or become _Unstuck_ from reality until the end of the scene. When an _Unstuck_ target spends Hope or marks Armor Slots, HP, or Stress, they must double the amount spent or marked.

features.name: Unreal Form
features.text: When the Abomination takes damage, reduce it by **1d20**. If the Abomination marks 1 or fewer Hit Points from a successful attack against them, you gain a Fear.

adversaries.name: Outer Realms Corruptor
adversaries.description: A shifting, formless mass seemingly made of chromatic light.
adversaries.motives: Confuse, distract, overwhelm

features.name: Will-Shattering Touch
features.text: When a PC takes damage from the Corruptor, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Disgorge Reality Flotsam
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to spew partially digested portions of consumed realities at all targets within Close range. Targets must succeed on a Knowledge Reaction Roll or mark 2 Stress.

adversaries.name: Outer Realms Thrall
adversaries.description: A vaguely humanoid form stripped of memory and identity.
adversaries.motives: Destroy, disgust, disorient, intimidate

features.name: Minion (13)
features.text: The Thrall is defeated when they take any damage. For every 13 damage a PC deals to the Thrall, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to choose a target and spotlight all Outer Realm Thralls within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 11 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

adversaries.name: Perfected Zombie
adversaries.description: A towering, muscular zombie with magically infused strength and skill.
adversaries.motives: Consume, hound, maim, terrify

features.name: Terrifying
features.text: When the Zombie makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Fearsome Presence
features.text: PCs can't spend Hope to use features against the Zombie.

features.name: Perfect Strike
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to make a standard attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Zombie succeeds against are _Vulnerable_ until their next rest.

features.name: Skilled Opportunist
features.text: When another adversary deals damage to a target within Very Close range of the Zombie, you can **spend a Fear** to add the Zombie's standard attack damage to the damage roll.

adversaries.name: Volcanic Dragon: Ashen Tyrant
adversaries.description: No enemy has ever had the insolence to wound the dragon so. As the lava settles, it's ground to ash like the dragon's past foes.
adversaries.motives: Choke, fly, intimidate, kill or be killed

features.name: Relentless (4)
features.text: The Ashen Tyrant can be spotlighted up to four times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Cornered
features.text: **Mark a Stress** instead of spending a Fear to spotlight the Ashen Tyrant.

features.name: Injured Wings
features.text: While flying, the Ashen Tyrant gains a +1 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Ashes to Ashes
features.text: When a PC rolls a failure while within Close range of the Ashen Tyrant, they lose a Hope and you gain a Fear. If the PC can't lose a Hope, they must mark a HP.

features.name: Desperate Rampage
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Ashen Tyrant succeeds against take **2d20+2** physical damage, are knocked back to Close range of where they were, and must mark a Stress.

features.name: Ashen Cloud
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to smash the ground and kick up ash within Far range. While within the ash cloud, a target has disadvantage on action rolls. The ash cloud clears the next time an adversary is spotlighted.

features.name: Apocalyptic Thrashing
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to activate. It ticks down when a PC rolls with Fear. When it triggers, the Ashen Tyrant thrashes about, causing environmental damage (such as an earthquake, avalanche, or collapsing walls). All targets within Far range must make a Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take **2d10+10** physical damage and are _Restrained_ by the rubble until they break free with a successful Strength Roll. Targets who succeed take half damage. If the Ashen Tyrant is defeated while this countdown is active, trigger the countdown immediately as the destruction caused by their death throes.

adversaries.name: Volcanic Dragon: Molten Scourge
adversaries.description: Enraged by their wounds, the dragon bursts into molten lava.
adversaries.motives: Douse with lava, incinerate, repel Invaders, reposition

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.text: The Molten Scourge can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Cracked Scales
features.text: When the Molten Scourge takes damage, roll a number of **d6s** equal to HP marked. For each result of 4 or higher, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Shattering Might
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target takes **4d8+1** physical damage, loses a Hope, and is knocked back to Close range. The Molten Scourge clears a Stress.

features.name: Eruption
features.text: **Spend a Fear** to erupt lava from beneath the Molten Scourge's scales, filling the area within Very Close range with molten lava. All targets in that area must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take **4d6+6** physical damage and be knocked back to Close range. This area remains lava. When a creature other than the Molten Scourge enters that area or acts while inside of it, they must mark 6 HP.

features.name: Volcanic Breath
features.text: When the Molten Scourge takes Major damage, roll a **d10**. On a result of 8 or higher, the Molten Scourge breathes a flow of lava in front of them within Far range. All targets in that area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take **2d10+4** physical damage, mark **1d4 Stress**, and are _Vulnerable_ until they clear a Stress. Targets who succeed take half damage and must mark a Stress.

features.name: Lava Splash
features.text: When the Molten Scourge takes Severe damage from an attack within Very Close range, molten blood gushes from the wound and deals **2d10+4** direct physical damage to the attacker.

features.name: Ashen Vengeance (Phase Change)
features.text: When the Molten Scourge marks their last HP, replace them with the Ashen Tyrant and immediately spotlight them.

adversaries.name: Volcanic Dragon: Obsidian Predator
adversaries.description: A massive winged creature with obsidian scales and impossibly sharp claws.
adversaries.motives: Defend lair, dive-bomb, fly, hunt, intimidate

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.text: The Obsidian Predator can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Flying
features.text: While flying, the Obsidian Predator gains a +3 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Obsidian Scales
features.text: The Obsidian Predator is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Avalanche Tail
features.text: **Mark a Stress** to make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Obsidian Predator succeeds against take **4d6+4** physical damage and are knocked back to Far range and _Vulnerable_ until their next roll with Hope.

features.name: Dive-Bomb
features.text: If the Obsidian Predator is flying, **mark a Stress** to choose a point within Far range. Move to that point and make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Obsidian Predator succeeds against take **2d10+6** physical damage and must mark a Stress and lose a Hope.

features.name: Erupting Rage (Phase Change)
features.text: When the Obsidian Predator marks their last HP, replace them with the Molten Scourge and immediately spotlight them.

adversaries.name: Zombie Legion
adversaries.description: A large pack of undead, still powerful despite their rotting flesh.
adversaries.motives: Consume brain, shred flesh, surround

features.name: Horde (2d6+5)
features.text: When the Legion has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals **2d6+5** physical damage instead.

features.name: Unyielding
features.text: The Legion has resistance to physical damage.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.text: The Legion can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Overwhelm
features.text: When the Legion takes Minor damage from an attack within Melee range, you can **mark a Stress** to make a standard attack with advantage against the attacker.

adversaries.name: Minor Water Elemental
adversaries.description: A living surge of seawater formed amidst the storm.
adversaries.motives: Disperse, destabilize, overwhelm

features.name: Rushing Water
features.text: Mark a Stress to attack a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d4+2 physical damage and the target becomes Vulnerable until the end of their next action.

features.name: Surging Tide
features.text: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Elemental succeeds against are knocked back to Close range.

adversaries.name: Nullveil Arcanist
adversaries.description: A robed spellbreaker trained to unravel arcane forces, he bend magical currents to shield their allies and suffocate hostile spellcasting at its source.
adversaries.motives: Protect key allies, disrupt spellcasters, reposition to avoid pressure

features.name: Spellward
features.text: The Arcanist has resistance to magic damage.

features.name: Arcane Barrier
features.text: Mark a Stress and choose an ally within Far range to create a barrier around them. Until they next take damage, they gain one of the following effects: Resistance to physical damage Resistance to magic damage An ally can only benefit from one Arcane Barrier at a time. Applying a new effect removes the previous one.

features.name: Veil of Silence
features.text: Spend a Fear and choose a target within Far range. The target must make a Finesse Reaction Roll (14). On a failure, they become Supressed. The effect lasts until the Arcanist takes damage.

adversaries.name: Crimson Rite Acolyte
adversaries.description: A ritual conduit who sustains the cult through blood rites, shared suffering, and unwavering devotion.
adversaries.motives: Sustain allied cultists, exploit targets, preserve the Cult at any cost

features.name: Minion (7)
features.text: The Acolyte is defeated when they take any damage. For every 7 damage a PC deals to the Acolyte, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Group Attack
features.text: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Crimson Rite Acolyte within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 5 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

adversaries.name: Crimson Rite Conduit
adversaries.description: A ritual conduit who sustains the cult through blood rites, shared suffering, and unwavering devotion.
adversaries.motives: Sustain allied cultists, exploit targets, preserve the Cult at any cost

features.name: Communion of Pain
features.text: Spend a Fear to have an ally within Very Close range clear 1d4 HP. You can also mark a Stress to have them clear a Stress.

features.name: Feed the Spiral
features.text: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, if they are Spiraling, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Final Benediction
features.text: When the Conduit is defeated, an ally within Close range clears 1 HP.

adversaries.name: Crimson Rite Keeper
adversaries.description: A ritual leader who enforces sacred rites through blood and terror, breaking the minds of intruders while directing the cult’s assault from above.
adversaries.motives: Spread fear, fracture resolve, coordinate allied cultists

features.name: Crimson Wings
features.text: The Keeper can fly. While flying, it can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Chorus of Devotion
features.text: Spend a Fear to spotlight 1d4 allies within Far range. Attacks they make while spotlighted this way deal half damage.

features.name: Spiral of Terror
features.text: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to make the target Spiraling until the end of the scene.

features.name: Cult's Rebuke
features.text: When the Keeper marks 2 or more HP from an attack within Melee range, the attacker must mark a Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.text: When the Keeper makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

adversaries.name: Crimson Rite Martyr
adversaries.description: A heavily armored zealot who offers their own flesh and suffering to protect the Crimson Rite. Martyrs hurl themselves into battle without hesitation, holding enemies in place while the ritual reaches its climax.
adversaries.motives: Protect the Cult, lock down enemies, overwhelm nearby targets

features.name: Hymn of Violence
features.text: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Martyr. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Blood Offering
features.text: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against a single target with advantage. If they mark 3 or more HP, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Stand Before the Rite
features.text: If a PC within Melee range attempts to leave that range, they must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, they become Restrained temporarily.

features.name: Final Offering
features.text: When the Martyr is defeated, they make an attack against all targets within Melee range. On a success, the target takes 2d6+6 physical damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.text: When the Martyr makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

adversaries.name: Ironbound Legionary
adversaries.description: A disciplined frontline soldier trained to hold formation and outlast the enemy through sheer resilience and coordination.
adversaries.motives: Hold formation, protect allies, follow commands without hesitation

features.name: Shield Bash
features.text: Mark a Stress to force a target within Melee range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is knocked back to Very Close range and is Vulnerable until they next act. On a success, the target must mark a Stress.

features.name: Driving Strike
features.text: Make an attack against a Vulnerable target within Melee range. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 2d12+4 physical damage. If the target marks HP from this attack, they must also mark a Stress.

adversaries.name: Shadowbolt Skirmisher
adversaries.description: A crossbow ambusher who strikes from cover, then vanishes before retaliation.
adversaries.motives: Isolate targets, fire from shadows, free when pressured

features.name: Lucky
features.text: The Skirmisher can reroll a 1 on an action roll.

features.name: Unseen Strike
features.text: If the Skirmisher is Hidden when they make a successful standard attack against a target, it deals 2d8+3 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Maintain Distance
features.text: After making a standard attack, the Skirmisher can move anywhere within Far range.

features.name: Cloaked
features.text: Mark a Stress to have the Skirmisher become Hidden until its next attack. Attacks made while Hidden from this feature have advantage.

adversaries.name: Silkwing Devourer
adversaries.description: A massive cave-dwelling bat-spider hybrid with a swollen abdomen, grasping limbs, and a lamprey-like maw built to consume prey whole.
adversaries.motives: Isolate prey, feed voraciously, terrorize intruders

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.text: The Devourer can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Gliding Leap
features.text: The Devourer can mark a Stress to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Brute Shockwave
features.text: Mark a Stress. All targets within Close range must make a Strength Reaction Roll (14). On a failure, they are knocked off balance and become Vulnerable until their next roll with Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Devour
features.text: Spend a Fear and make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the target is grappled and pulled into the Devourer’s maw. While grappled this way, the target becomes Restrained and Vulnerable and takes 1d10+4 physical damage. An Ally can attempt to free them with a successful Strenght Roll (15).

adversaries.name: Silkwing Strider
adversaries.description: A lean cave-dwelling bat-spider hybrid with long legs and a serrated maw, bred to stalk tunnels and overwhelm prey.
adversaries.motives: Flank prey, chase intruders, defend the nest

features.name: Skittering Glide
features.text: The Strider can climb and walk on vertical surfaces and even upside down, additionally they can mark a Stress to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Flanking Tactics
features.text: When the Strider is within Melee range of a creature and another Silkwing is within Close range of that same creature, attacks against that creature have advantage.

features.name: Chittering Shriek
features.text: Mark 1 Stress to force all creatures within Very Close range to make an Presence Reaction Roll (14). On a failure, the next attack against the target has advantage.

adversaries.name: Spectral Hound
adversaries.description: A ghostly hunting hound bound to an ancient battlefield, phasing in and out of reality as it tracks its prey.
adversaries.motives: Stalk silently, isolate prey, strike from beyond the veil

features.name: Ghost
features.text: The Hound has resistance to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Phasing Stalker
features.text: While the Hound is not in direct line of sight of any PC, it is considered Hidden.

features.name: Spectral Ambush
features.text: While Hidden, mark a Stress to emerge into Melee range of a target within Very Close range. The target must succeed on an Agility or Instinct Reaction Roll (15) or take 2d8 magic damage and become temporarily Restrained.

features.name: Ghostly Roar
features.text: Spend a Fear to roar, causing a spectral energy to propagate. All targets within Close range must succeed on an Presence Reaction Roll (14) or take 2d8 magic damage and become Vulnerable temporarily.

adversaries.name: Gravemaw Juggernaut
adversaries.description: A massive, animate boulder with a crude, grinning face. It hurls itself across the battlefield with reckless force, each impact cracking its own unstable core.
adversaries.motives: Crush targets through repeated impacts, build momentum, ignore self-preservation

features.name: Compressed Mass
features.text: The Gravemaw Juggernaut has resistance to physical damage. Damage dealt to the Juggernaut does not reduce its Stress.

features.name: Runaway Momentum
features.text: Each time the Juggernaut moves or is forced to move, it must mark a Stress.

features.name: Crushing Impact
features.text: Make a standard attack. On a success, if the Juggernaut moved before this attack, increase the damage by 1d10.

features.name: Unstable Core
features.text: When the Juggernaut marks its last Stress, it explodes and is defeated. Each creature within Close range must make an Agility Reaction Roll (16) or take 2d10 physical damage.
